How It Works When you run docfx docfx_project/docfx.json from the repository root, DocFX will: Scan the C# projects specified in docfx.json (configured to look for src/**/*.csproj) Extract XML documentation comments from your code Generate API reference documentation in this directory Create a toc.yml file that organizes the API documentation Important Notes Do not manually edit generated DocFX output files in this folder (such as *.yml and toc.yml) — they will be overwritten each time you run the DocFX build Hand-authored files like index.md and this README.md are intentionally maintained by hand and will be preserved across DocFX runs The actual API reference metadata files (*.yml files) will be generated automatically

api/Wolfgang.D20.AverageRollExtensions.h.title: Class AverageRollExtensions | Wolfgang.D20.Dice Documentation
api/Wolfgang.D20.AverageRollExtensions.h.summary: Class AverageRollExtensions Namespace Wolfgang.D20 Assembly Wolfgang.D20.Dice.dll Extension methods that compute the average roll of a die or collection of dice, rounded to a whole number. public static class AverageRollExtensions Inheritance object AverageRollExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The average roll is the midpoint of the possible results, (MinValue + MaxValue) / 2, which is always a whole number or a half. These methods round that midpoint to an int, which is convenient for tabletop rules that call for average damage rounded a particular way — for example, half damage on a successful save is conventionally rounded down. Methods AverageRoundedDown(IDice) Returns the average roll of the dice, including the modifier, rounded down to the nearest whole number. public static int AverageRoundedDown(this IDice dice) Parameters dice IDice The dice to average. Returns int The midpoint of MinValue and MaxValue, rounded down. Examples // 2d6 averages 7; half damage on a successful save, rounded down, is 3. int half = new Dice(2, 6).AverageRoundedDown() / 2; // 3 Exceptions ArgumentNullException dice is null. AverageRoundedDown(IDie) Returns the average roll of the die, rounded down to the nearest whole number. public static int AverageRoundedDown(this IDie die) Parameters die IDie The die to average. Returns int The midpoint of MinValue and MaxValue, rounded down. Examples int average = new Die(6).AverageRoundedDown(); // 3.5 -> 3 Exceptions ArgumentNullException die is null. AverageRoundedUp(IDice) Returns the average roll of the dice, including the modifier, rounded up to the nearest whole number. public static int AverageRoundedUp(this IDice dice) Parameters dice IDice The dice to average. Returns int The midpoint of MinValue and MaxValue, rounded up. Examples int average = new Dice(2, 6).AverageRoundedUp(); // 7.0 -> 7 Exceptions ArgumentNullException dice is null. AverageRoundedUp(IDie) Returns the average roll of the die, rounded up to the nearest whole number. public static int AverageRoundedUp(this IDie die) Parameters die IDie The die to average. Returns int The midpoint of MinValue and MaxValue, rounded up. Examples int average = new Die(6).AverageRoundedUp(); // 3.5 -> 4 Exceptions ArgumentNullException die is null.

api/Wolfgang.D20.Dice.html.title: Class Dice | Wolfgang.D20.Dice Documentation
api/Wolfgang.D20.Dice.html.summary: Class Dice Namespace Wolfgang.D20 Assembly Wolfgang.D20.Dice.dll Represents a collection of Die rolled together, with an optional flat modifier. public sealed class Dice : IDice, ICollection<Die>, IEnumerable<Die>, IEnumerable, IEquatable<Dice> Inheritance object Dice Implements IDice ICollection<Die> IEnumerable<Die> IEnumerable IEquatable<Dice> Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods AverageRollExtensions.AverageRoundedDown(IDice) AverageRollExtensions.AverageRoundedUp(IDice) Remarks The dice need not be homogeneous; a single Dice can contain dice with differing side counts, for example 2d6+1d4+3. Individual dice can be added and removed after construction. Constructors Dice(IEnumerable<Die>, int) Constructs a new instance of Dice from an existing sequence of Die and an optional modifier. public Dice(IEnumerable<Die> dice, int modifier = 0) Parameters dice IEnumerable<Die> The dice to include in the collection modifier int An optional modifier to add to the result Examples // 1d6+1d4+1: a heterogeneous pool built from individual dice. var dice = new Dice(new[] { new Die(6), new Die(4) }, modifier: 1); Exceptions ArgumentNullException dice is null ArgumentException dice contains a null element Dice(int, int, int) Constructs a new instance of Dice containing the specified number of identical dice and an optional modifier. public Dice(int dieCount = 1, int sideCount = 6, int modifier = 0) Parameters dieCount int The number of dice sideCount int The number of sides on each die modifier int An optional modifier to add to the result Examples // 2d6+3: two six-sided dice plus a flat +3. var attack = new Dice(dieCount: 2, sideCount: 6, modifier: 3); int total = attack.Roll(); // a value in [5, 15] Remarks This convenience constructor builds a homogeneous collection of dieCount dice, each with sideCount sides. A sideCount of 2 represents a coin toss. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException dieCount is less than 1 ArgumentOutOfRangeException sideCount is less than 2 Properties Count The number of dice in the collection. ICollection<T> implementation; equal to DieCount. public int Count { get; } Property Value int DieCount The number of dice in the collection. public int DieCount { get; } Property Value int IsReadOnly Always false; the collection is mutable. ICollection<T> implementation. public bool IsReadOnly { get; } Property Value bool MaxValue The maximum value that can be rolled with the dice in the collection and the modifier. public int MaxValue { get; } Property Value int MinValue The minimum value that can be rolled with the dice in the collection and the modifier. public int MinValue { get; } Property Value int Modifier An optional modifier to add to the total of the dice rolled. public int Modifier { get; set; } Property Value int Examples var dice = new Dice(1, 20) { Modifier = 5 }; // 1d20+5 int total = dice.Roll(); // a value in [6, 25] Remarks The value can be positive or negative, and can be used to adjust the result of the roll. Methods Add(Die) Adds a die to the collection. public void Add(Die item) Parameters item Die The die to add. Exceptions ArgumentNullException item is null Clear() Removes all dice from the collection. The Modifier is left unchanged. public void Clear() Contains(Die) Determines whether the collection contains a die with the same number of sides. public bool Contains(Die item) Parameters item Die The die to locate. Returns bool true if a matching die is found; otherwise, false. CopyTo(Die[], int) Copies the dice in the collection to an array, starting at the specified index. public void CopyTo(Die[] array, int arrayIndex) Parameters array Die[] The destination array. arrayIndex int The zero-based index in array at which copying begins. Equals(object?) Checks if this instance is equal to another object. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object The object to compare with this instance. It can be null or of any type. Returns bool True if the object is a Dice instance equal to this instance; otherwise, false. Equals(Dice?) Checks if this instance is equal to another instance of Dice. public bool Equals(Dice? other) Parameters other Dice The other instance of Dice to compare with this instance. Returns bool True if both instances have the same Modifier and the same multiset of dice (matching side counts, regardless of order); otherwise, false. GetEnumerator() Returns an enumerator that iterates through the dice in the collection. public IEnumerator<Die> GetEnumerator() Returns IEnumerator<Die> An enumerator over the dice. GetHashCode() Generates a hash code for this instance based on its Modifier and the multiset of dice side counts (order-independent). public override int GetHashCode() Returns int A combined hash code. Remarks Dice is a mutable collection: dice can be added or removed and Modifier is settable, and both feed into this hash code (consistent with Equals(Dice?)). As with any mutable type, do not mutate a Dice instance while it is being used as a key in a hashed collection such as Dictionary<TKey, TValue> or HashSet<T> — doing so leaves the instance in the wrong bucket and breaks subsequent lookups. If you need a stable key, snapshot the value first (for example the result of ToString()). Remove(Die) Removes the first occurrence of a die with the same number of sides from the collection. public bool Remove(Die item) Parameters item Die The die to remove. Returns bool true if a matching die was removed; otherwise, false. RemoveAt(int) Removes the die at the specified index in the collection. public void RemoveAt(int index) Parameters index int The zero-based index of the die to remove. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException index is less than 0 or not less than DieCount. Roll() Rolls every die in the collection and returns the total value rolled, including any modifier. public int Roll() Returns int The sum of an independent roll of each die in the collection plus Modifier. Always between MinValue and MaxValue inclusive. Examples var dice = new Dice(3, 6); // 3d6 int total = dice.Roll(); // a value in [3, 18] ToString() Returns a string representation of the dice in standard dice notation, for example 2d6+1d4+3. public override string ToString() Returns string The dice grouped by side count (in the order each side count first appears), followed by the modifier. Dice order is not significant. The modifier is omitted when zero, and a negative modifier renders with a leading minus sign. An empty collection renders only the modifier (or an empty string when the modifier is zero). Examples string notation = new Dice(2, 6, 3).ToString(); // "2d6+3" TryParse(string?) Tries to parse a string representation of dice notation into a Dice instance. public static Result<Dice?> TryParse(string? notation) Parameters notation string The dice notation to parse, for example 2d6+1d4+3. Whitespace is ignored. The notation may contain any number of dice terms (XdY) and flat modifiers (+Z / -Z). Returns Result<Dice> A Wolfgang.TryPattern.Result<T> containing the parsed Dice instance if successful; otherwise, a failed result with Wolfgang.TryPattern.Result.ErrorMessage describing the failure. Accessing Wolfgang.TryPattern.Result<T>.Value on a failed result throws InvalidOperationException. Examples var result = Dice.TryParse("2d6+3"); if (result.Succeeded) { Dice dice = result.Value!; int total = dice.Roll(); // a value in [5, 15] }

api/Wolfgang.D20.Die.html.title: Class Die | Wolfgang.D20.Dice Documentation
api/Wolfgang.D20.Die.html.summary: Class Die Namespace Wolfgang.D20 Assembly Wolfgang.D20.Dice.dll Represents a single die with a fixed number of sides. public sealed class Die : IDie, IEquatable<Die> Inheritance object Die Implements IDie IEquatable<Die> Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods AverageRollExtensions.AverageRoundedDown(IDie) AverageRollExtensions.AverageRoundedUp(IDie) Constructors Die(int) Constructs a new instance of Die with the specified number of sides. public Die(int sideCount = 6) Parameters sideCount int The number of sides on the die Examples var d20 = new Die(20); int result = d20.Roll(); // a value in [1, 20] Remarks sideCount of 2 represents a coin toss Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException sideCount is less than 2 Properties MaxValue The maximum value that can be rolled on the die. Equal to SideCount. public int MaxValue { get; } Property Value int MinValue The minimum value that can be rolled on the die. Always 1. public int MinValue { get; } Property Value int SideCount The number of sides on the die. Must be at least 2. public int SideCount { get; } Property Value int Remarks A value of 2 represents a coin toss, 3 represents a three-sided die, etc. Methods Equals(object?) Checks if this instance is equal to another object. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object The object to compare with this instance. It can be null or of any type. Returns bool True if the object is a Die instance with the same SideCount; otherwise, false. Equals(Die?) Checks if this instance is equal to another instance of Die. public bool Equals(Die? other) Parameters other Die The other instance of Die to compare with this instance. Returns bool True if both instances have the same SideCount; otherwise, false. GetHashCode() Generates a hash code for this instance based on its SideCount. public override int GetHashCode() Returns int A hash code derived from SideCount. Roll() Rolls the die and returns the value rolled. public int Roll() Returns int A uniform random value in [MinValue, MaxValue] inclusive. Examples var d6 = new Die(6); int result = d6.Roll(); // a value in [1, 6] ToString() Returns a string representation of the die in the format "dY" where Y is the number of sides. public override string ToString() Returns string The die in standard dY notation. Examples string notation = new Die(20).ToString(); // "d20"

api/Wolfgang.D20.IDice.html.title: Interface IDice | Wolfgang.D20.Dice Documentation
api/Wolfgang.D20.IDice.html.summary: Interface IDice Namespace Wolfgang.D20 Assembly Wolfgang.D20.Dice.dll Represents a collection of Die rolled together, with an optional flat modifier. public interface IDice Extension Methods AverageRollExtensions.AverageRoundedDown(IDice) AverageRollExtensions.AverageRoundedUp(IDice) Examples IDice dice = new Dice(2, 6, 3); // 2d6+3 int total = dice.Roll(); // a value in [5, 15] Remarks The dice need not be homogeneous; a single Dice can contain dice with differing side counts, for example 2d6+1d4+3. Properties DieCount The number of dice in the collection. int DieCount { get; } Property Value int MaxValue The maximum value that can be rolled with the dice in the collection and the modifier. int MaxValue { get; } Property Value int MinValue The minimum value that can be rolled with the dice in the collection and the modifier. int MinValue { get; } Property Value int Modifier An optional modifier to add to the total of the dice rolled. int Modifier { get; } Property Value int Remarks The value can be positive or negative, and can be used to adjust the result of the roll. Methods Roll() Rolls every die in the collection and returns the total value rolled, including any modifier. int Roll() Returns int The sum of an independent roll of each die in the collection plus Modifier. Always between MinValue and MaxValue inclusive. ToString() Returns a string representation of the dice in standard dice notation, for example 2d6+1d4+3. string ToString() Returns string The dice grouped by side count, followed by the modifier; dice order is not significant, and the modifier (omitted when zero, leading minus when negative) always comes last.

docs/getting-started.html.title: Getting Started | Wolfgang.D20.Dice Documentation
docs/getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started This guide will help you quickly get up and running with Wolfgang.D20.Dice. Prerequisites The .NET 10.0 SDK is required to build the highest TFM. Building the net462 target additionally requires the .NET Framework 4.6.2 reference assemblies / targeting pack (typically installed via Visual Studio's ".NET desktop development" workload on Windows; the SDK alone does not include them, and net462 builds are Windows-only). The netstandard2.0 and net8.0 targets build with the .NET 10 SDK alone on any OS. The library ships against net462, netstandard2.0, net8.0, and net10.0. Installation Via NuGet Package Manager dotnet add package Wolfgang.D20.Dice Via Package Manager Console Install-Package Wolfgang.D20.Dice Quick Start using Wolfgang.D20; // Roll a single d20 var d20 = new Dice(dieCount: 1, sideCount: 20); int result = d20.Roll(); Console.WriteLine($"Rolled {d20}: {result}"); // e.g. "Rolled 1d20: 14" // Roll 2d6 with a +3 modifier var attackRoll = new Dice(dieCount: 2, sideCount: 6, modifier: 3); Console.WriteLine($"Attack ({attackRoll}): {attackRoll.Roll()}"); Console.WriteLine($"Range: {attackRoll.MinValue}–{attackRoll.MaxValue}"); // Parse dice notation from a string var parseResult = Dice.TryParse("1d20+5"); if (parseResult.Succeeded) { Console.WriteLine($"Parsed: {parseResult.Value} → {parseResult.Value.Roll()}"); } Next Steps Explore the API Reference for detailed documentation Read the Introduction to learn more about Wolfgang.D20.Dice Check out example projects in the GitHub repository Common Issues Parsing fails for valid-looking notation Dice.TryParse expects the format XdY, XdY+Z, or XdY-Z where X is optional (defaults to 1). The die count must be at least 1 and the side count at least 2. Check result.ErrorMessage for details on why parsing failed. Modifier can be negative A negative modifier is valid — new Dice(1, 20, -2) creates 1d20-2. The MinValue property accounts for the modifier, so it may be less than 1. Additional Resources GitHub Repository Contributing Guidelines Report an Issue

docs/introduction.html.title: Introduction | Wolfgang.D20.Dice Documentation
docs/introduction.html.summary: Introduction Welcome to Wolfgang.D20.Dice! Overview Wolfgang.D20.Dice is a .NET library for simulating tabletop dice rolls using standard dice notation (e.g., 2d6+3). It provides a strongly-typed Dice class with roll generation, notation parsing, and value-equality semantics. Key Features Dice Notation — standard XdY+Z format with any die count, side count, and modifier Parsing — Dice.TryParse("1d20+5") with full validation, returning a Result<T> from Wolfgang.TryPattern Roll Generation — Roll() produces a random result within the valid range Min/Max Calculation — MinValue and MaxValue computed from the dice configuration Equality — full IEquatable<Dice> and IEqualityComparer<Dice> implementation ToString — formats back to dice notation (2d6+3, 1d20, 3d8-2) Getting Help If you need help with Wolfgang.D20.Dice, please: Check the Getting Started guide Review the API Reference Visit the GitHub repository Open an issue on GitHub Issues
